keyPointsSummary: 'Have to' is used with I, you, we, they; 'Has to' is used with he, she, it
Shows obligation that comes from outside (rules, circumstances), not personal opinion
Negative: do not have to / does not have to (means 'not necessary', not prohibition)

quickQuiz.hindi: मुझे रोज़ ऑफिस जाना पड़ता है।
quickQuiz.answer: I have to go to office every day.

quickQuiz.hindi: उसे यह काम पूरा करना पड़ता है।
quickQuiz.answer: He has to complete this work.

quickQuiz.hindi: हमें नियमों का पालन करना पड़ता है।
quickQuiz.answer: We have to follow the rules.

quickQuiz.hindi: उसे रोज़ जल्दी उठना पड़ता है।
quickQuiz.answer: She has to wake up early every day.

quickQuiz.hindi: क्या तुम्हें यह करना पड़ता है?
quickQuiz.answer: Do you have to do this?

quickQuiz.hindi: उसे बहुत मेहनत करनी पड़ती है।
quickQuiz.answer: He has to work very hard.

quickQuiz.hindi: मुझे यह पैसे देने पड़ते हैं।
quickQuiz.answer: I have to pay this money.
We have to fill the form.

quickQuiz.hindi: तुम्हें ऐसा नहीं करना पड़ता (जरूरी नहीं है)।
quickQuiz.answer: You do not have to do this.

mustRememberRules: 'Have to' is used with I, you, we, they; 'Has to' is used with he, she, it
Shows obligation that comes from outside (rules, circumstances), not personal opinion
Negative: do not have to / does not have to (means 'not necessary', not prohibition)
